title: Ordered Pairs in All Four Quadrants
contentObjective: I can plot ordered pairs in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane.
languageObjective: I can explain my work using the words quadrant, negative coordinate, reflection, and axis.

vocabulary.definition: One of four sections of the coordinate plane, numbered I through IV counterclockwise from the top right
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una de las cuatro secciones del plano cartesiano, numeradas del I al IV en sentido contrario a las manecillas del reloj desde la parte superior derecha
vocabulary.visual: I (+, +) top-right, II (-, +) top-left, III (-, -) bottom-left, IV (+, -) bottom-right
vocabulary.cloze: One of the four regions of the coordinate plane, numbered I to IV, is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A coordinate value less than zero; negative x means left of the origin, negative y means below the origin
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un valor de coordenada menor que cero; x negativa significa a la izquierda del origen, y negativa significa debajo del origen
vocabulary.visual: (-3, 2) means 3 left and 2 up from the origin
vocabulary.cloze: A coordinate less than zero, which places a point left of or below the origin, is a ___.

examples.why: It is a coordinate less than zero.

vocabulary.definition: A mirror image of a point across an axis
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una imagen reflejada de un punto a través de un eje
vocabulary.visual: (3, 2) reflected over the y-axis is (-3, 2) — same distance, opposite side
vocabulary.cloze: A flip of a point over an axis is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A reference line on the coordinate plane; the x-axis is horizontal and the y-axis is vertical
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una línea de referencia en el plano cartesiano; el eje x es horizontal y el eje y es vertical
vocabulary.visual: The x-axis goes left-right; the y-axis goes up-down; they cross at (0, 0)
vocabulary.cloze: One of the two number lines, the x-axis or y-axis, that form the grid is an ___.

vocabulary.definition: Whole numbers and their opposites, including zero
vocabulary.definitionEs: Los números enteros y sus opuestos, incluyendo el cero
vocabulary.cloze: A whole number or its opposite is an ___.

launch.narrative: Captain Vega realized her treasure map only covered one corner of the island! She needs to expand the map in all directions — north, south, east, and west of the base camp at the origin (0, 0). Now clues can have negative coordinates: (-4, 3) means 4 units west and 3 units north. A clue at (2, -5) means 2 units east and 5 units south. Which quadrant is each clue in?
launch.contextImage: Full island treasure map with coordinate grid extending in all four directions from a central base camp
noticePrompts: How do you know which quadrant a point is in by looking at the signs of its coordinates?
What do negative x-values and negative y-values mean on the map?
Where is the point (0, 0) on the expanded map?
wonderPrompts: Can a point be in more than one quadrant?
What quadrant is a point on if one of its coordinates is zero?

explore.instructions: Plot each treasure clue on the expanded map and identify which quadrant it falls in.
explore.label: Plot each point and label its quadrant.

discourse.prompt: How do the signs of the x- and y-coordinates tell you which quadrant a point is in?
discourse.sentenceFrame: In Quadrant ___, the x-coordinate is ___ and the y-coordinate is ___. I know (-3, -4) is in Quadrant ___ because both coordinates are ___.

optional.stem: In which quadrant is the point (-4, -6)?
optional.explanation: Negative x and negative y place a point in Quadrant III.

optional.stem: Point A is (5, 3). Which point shares the same x-coordinate?
Which ordered pair is in Quadrant IV?
optional.explanation: Quadrant IV has positive x and negative y: (8, -3).

optional.stem: The point (0, -7) lies on which axis?
optional.explanation: With x = 0, the point is on the y-axis.

approaching.stem: In which quadrant is the point (-4, 5)?
approaching.explanation: The x-coordinate is negative (left) and the y-coordinate is positive (up). Left and up is Quadrant II.

approaching.stem: A point has a positive x-coordinate and a negative y-coordinate. Which quadrant is it in?
approaching.explanation: Positive x (right) and negative y (down) = Quadrant IV (bottom-right).

approaching.stem: Which point is located on the x-axis?
approaching.explanation: A point is on the x-axis when its y-coordinate is 0. (4, 0) is on the x-axis.

approaching.label: Match each ordered pair to its quadrant.
Sort these points by their quadrant: I first, then II, III, IV.

onLevel.label: Plot one point in each quadrant: (3, 2), (-4, 1), (-2, -3), (5, -4)

onLevel.instructions: For each ordered pair, write the signs of x and y and name the quadrant (or axis).

onLevel.label: Match each quadrant to its coordinate sign pattern.

onLevel.stem: Point A is at (-5, 3). If you move 8 units to the right, what are the new coordinates?
onLevel.explanation: Moving right means adding to the x-coordinate: -5 + 8 = 3. The y stays the same. New point: (3, 3).

extending.title: Find the Quadrant Error

workedExample.label: Student thinking
workedExample.work: The x is -3, so I go left 3. The y is -5, so I go up 5.

extending.correctWork: A negative y-coordinate means DOWN, not up. Go left 3 and DOWN 5. The point (-3, -5) is in Quadrant III (both negative), not Quadrant II.
hints: What direction does a NEGATIVE y-coordinate go — up or down?
Both coordinates are negative. In which quadrant are both x and y negative?

extending.prompt: A point starts in Quadrant I at (4, 6). Describe a move that puts it in Quadrant III. What must happen to both coordinates? Can you give the exact new coordinates after your move?
extending.sentenceFrame: To move from Quadrant I to Quadrant III, both coordinates must become ___. Starting at (4, 6), I could move ___ units left and ___ units down to reach (___,___), which is in Quadrant III because ___.

extending.label: Plot these 4 points and connect them in order to form a shape. Name the shape and identify which quadrants it spans: (-3, 2), (3, 2), (3, -2), (-3, -2)

connect.scenario: A city uses a coordinate grid to map locations. City Hall is at the origin (0, 0). The library is at (-3, 4), the park is at (5, -2), the school is at (2, 3), and the hospital is at (-4, -1).
connect.promptQuestion: How do four-quadrant coordinates help a city map locations in every direction from the center?
connect.prompt: The library is in Quadrant ___ because its x is ___ and its y is ___. The park is in Quadrant ___ because ___. Four-quadrant coordinates help because ___.

exitTicket.stem: A point has a negative x-coordinate and a negative y-coordinate. Which quadrant is it in?
exitTicket.explanation: Quadrant III is where both x and y are negative. This means the point is left of the origin and below the origin.
